notes: Name of Kael'thas' pet phoenix god, Al'ar.
Used in 'Selama ashal'anore' (Justice for our great people).
Contextual from 'Bal'a dash' (Greetings/Peace be with you).
Central concept of Blood Elf culture. 'Anu belore dela'na' (The sun guides us).
Sin'dorei (Children of Blood), Quel'dorei (High Elves), Kal'dorei (Night Elves).
Quel'Thalas (High Kingdom), Quel'dorei (High Elves), Quel'Serrar (High Blade).
Selama ashal'anore (Justice for our great people).
Bash'a no falor talah! (Kael'thas combat quote).
Ru shanna shal'dorei (The nightborne will survive)
Erana-dora isil (A thousand thanks upon you)
Midnight (12.0) Alpha: Vereesa cries this seeing Rhonin's death
Midnight (12.0) Alpha: Halduron mentions Light drove them to zealotry
Extrapolated from Kael'thas casting Flamestrike: 'Felomin ashal!' (Felo = fire, ashal = great, min = blast)
Extrapolated from Nethermancer Sepethrea dying quote: 'Anu bala belore alon!' (Hail the sun's eternal blessing)
Extrapolated from Prince Keleseth summoning skeletons: 'Aranal, ledel!' (Rise, dead ones!)
Phonotactically generated (contrast with anaria - formal speak)
Lura (Sea) from Voyage to the Sunken City
From anu (self) + ore (one/being), contraction al'ore = 'the self'
il- prefix denoting second person, modeled on al'ore pattern
From anu (self) + dorei (children/people) = 'our selves, we the people'
Short demonstrative proximal; soft vowel-initial, typical Thalassian deictic
Distal demonstrative; vowel contrast e/o marks proximal/distal
Interrogative animate; th- onset common in Thalassian question words
Interrogative inanimate; parallels theli with -ra ending for things vs -li for persons
Negation particle; soft nasal onset, echoes shindu (failing) in semantic field
Totality marker; resonates with thalas (home/hearth = totality of dwelling)
New root for abundance; sh-onset follows Thalassian phonotactics
Base numeral; also used as 'being/individual' in compounds
Base numeral two; diphthong -ai marks duality
Evokes elongation; -iel suffix common in Thalassian adjectives
New root for woman; soft sibilant + liquid, distinctly elvish
From new root roval (wing/flight) + -in (one who) = 'winged one'
New root; soft r- onset + sh cluster, loyal-companion connotation
Compound: thas (forest) + ore (one) = 'one of the forest, a single tree'
New root; sor- evokes origin/source, -in singulative
New root for skin/hide; soft consonants reth- typical Thalassian
New root; echoes osseous, fits Thalassian vowel-heavy pattern
New root; sil- evokes silk/thread, -aren noun suffix
From dris (eye/sight) + al (the/of) = 'the seeing one, crown of body'
New root for ear; thel- (sense/perceive) + -is body-part suffix
New fundamental root for eye/sight; used in many compounds
New root; rash- (breath/air sense) + -is body-part suffix
New root for mouth; or- (voice/speech) + -is body suffix
New root for foot; tal- (path/walk) + -or agent-like suffix
New root for hand; eth- (craft/grasp) + -is body suffix
New root; bright vowels evoke joy, el- (light/bright) + -ara
From anore (people/nation) + ael (action) = 'to be of the people, live'
